perturbation.original_text: Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Agreement are defined in Appendix A. Appendix A also contains usage rules that apply to this Agreement. Appendix A is incorporated by reference into this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Agreement are defined throughout this document. All terms and definitions are subject to change at the discretion of the servicer.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the terms are defined throughout the document, but removing 'Appendix A', there is an inconsistency with the original agreement that defines the key terms in the missing appendix. The clause 'All terms and definitions are subject to change at the discretion of the servicer' introduces uncertainty and possible contradiction with other clauses that might rely on a fixed definition of terms. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the contract in Section 2.4 still refers to these key definitions while they have been ambiguously altered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations and Warranties About Depositor Transferred Property. (a) Representations and Warranties About Pool of Receivables. The Depositor makes the following representations and warranties about the pool of Receivables on which the Issuer is relying in acquiring the Depositor Transferred Property. The representations and warranties are made as of the Closing Date (for the Initial Receivables) and as of each Acquisition Date (for the related Additional Receivables) and will survive the transfer and absolute assignment of the Depositor Transferred Property by the Depositor to the Issuer under this Agreement and the pledge of the Depositor Transferred Property by the Issuer to the Indenture Trustee under the Indenture.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that capitalized terms are defined in Appendix A, which is incorporated by reference. The changed text states that these terms are defined throughout the document and are subject to change at the servicer's discretion. This contradicts the contradicted text, 'Representations and Warranties About Depositor Transferred Property' in Section 2.4, which relies on specific definitions of terms. The servicer's ability to unilaterally change definitions creates an in-text contradiction because it undermines the representations and warranties made by the Depositor regarding the pool of Receivables. The changed text introduces ambiguity and potential for inconsistency in the contract, making the representations and warranties less reliable and enforceable. This adheres to the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: On or before the Closing Date, the Servicer will notify the Indenture Trustee and the Owner Trustee and provide a specimen signature of each Person who (a) is authorized to give instructions and directions to the Indenture Trustee and the Owner Trustee on behalf of the Servicer and (b) is a Responsible Person for the Servicer. The Servicer may change such Persons at any time by notifying the Indenture Trustee and the Owner Trustee.
perturbation.changed_text: The Servicer will designate individuals authorized to provide instructions to the Indenture Trustee and the Owner Trustee. Contact information for these individuals will be maintained internally and provided upon request.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement to provide a specimen signature and notify the Indenture Trustee and Owner Trustee directly creates a contradiction. Section 10.3 requires notices to be addressed to recipients at their stated address, but there is no longer a guaranteed central registry of contacts. The phrase 'provided upon request' introduces ambiguity and there is no requirement of when they should be providing the information upon request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notices. (a) Notices to Parties. All notices, requests, directions, consents, waivers or other communications to or from the parties must be in writing and will be considered received by the recipient: (i) for personally delivered, express or certified mail or courier, when received; (ii) for a fax, when receipt is confirmed by telephone, reply email or reply fax from the recipient; (iii) for an email, when receipt is confirmed by telephone or reply email from the recipient; and (iv) for an electronic posting to a password-protected website to which the recipient has access, on delivery of an email (without the requirement of confirmation of receipt) stating that the electronic posting has been made.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Servicer to notify the Indenture Trustee and Owner Trustee of authorized individuals and provide specimen signatures. The changed text removes this notification requirement and replaces it with an internal contact list provided only 'upon request.' This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Notices' section, which mandates that notices be sent to specific addresses to be considered received. Without a guaranteed central registry of contacts as initially mandated, there's no reliable way to ensure notices reach the appropriate individuals. The phrase 'provided upon request' introduces ambiguity and lacks a requirement of when they should be providing the information upon request, further conflicting with the notice requirement in the contradicted text. Therefore, the modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any Person (a) into which the Depositor is merged or consolidated, (b) resulting from a merger or consolidation to which the Depositor is a party, (c) succeeding to the Depositor's business or (d) that is an Affiliate of the Depositor to whom the Depositor has assigned this Agreement, will be the successor to the Depositor under this Agreement. Within fifteen (15) Business Days after the merger, consolidation, succession or assignment, such Person will (i) execute an agreement to assume the Depositor's obligations under this Agreement and each Transaction Document to which the Depositor is a party (unless the assumption happens by operation of Law), (ii) deliver to the Issuer, the Owner Trustee and the Indenture Trustee an Officer's Certificate and an Opinion of Counsel each stating that the merger, consolidation, succession or assignment and the assumption agreement comply with this Section 5.3, (iii) deliver to the Issuer, the Owner Trustee and the Indenture Trustee an Opinion of Counsel stating that the security interest in favor of the Issuer in the Depositor Transferred Property and the Indenture Trustee in the Collateral is or will be perfected and (iv) notify the Rating Agencies of the merger, consolidation, succession or assignment.
perturbation.changed_text: The Depositor may be merged, consolidated, or succeeded by another entity. The resulting entity will be considered the successor to the Depositor under this Agreement. However, the Depositor is not required to notify any other party about the merger, consolidation, succession or assignment.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirements of executing an agreement to assume obligations, delivering officer's certificates and opinions of counsel, and notifying the rating agencies, creates a contradiction. Section 9.1 still requires the depositor to file financing statements and notify the Owner Trustee and the Indenture Trustee before a change in corporate structure if it could require the filing of a new financing statement. This requirement directly conflicts with the altered Section 5.3, which eliminates the obligation to notify anyone of such changes. Now the question is if such a change is even possible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Financing Statements. (a) Filing of Financing Statements. The Depositor will file financing and continuation statements, and amendments to the statements, in the jurisdictions and with the filing offices necessary to perfect the Issuer's interest in the Depositor Transferred Property. The Depositor will promptly deliver to the Issuer and the Indenture Trustee file-stamped copies of, or filing receipts for, any financing statement, continuation statement and amendment to a previously filed financing statement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.3 eliminates the depositor's obligation to notify other parties about mergers, consolidations, or successions. This directly contradicts Section 9.1, which requires the depositor to file financing statements and notify the Owner Trustee and the Indenture Trustee before a change in corporate structure if it could require the filing of a new financing statement. The original Section 5.3 ensured alignment, while the changed text creates a direct conflict, making it unclear whether the depositor is obligated to provide notice under Section 9.1 when Section 5.3 states no notification is needed.
